faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opponent team rendered with distinct color palette from player team

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player character recolored for title screen visibility in US version (changed from blended background to black background requiring contrast adjustment)
All player characters received visual touch-ups across regions; character sprites modified to ensure legibility and team distinction

environmental_storytelling.technique: Player name localization as cultural signaling
environmental_storytelling.example: Japanese names (Echenkaiser, Uma Kamado) replaced with Western names (Hansen, Juarez) in US/EU versions, signaling regional team composition expectations

environmental_storytelling.technique: Logo and UI color treatment for regional identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Jaleco logo recolored and realigned across regions, establishing publisher presence within game visual hierarchy

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visual distinction maintained through color and sprite refinement to communicate team affiliation during gameplay

notes: Goal! is a sports-soccer game with minimal narrative or political world-state communication. The primary visual communication systems are: (1) team differentiation through color palettes and sprite variation, (2) regional localization affecting visual presentation (background contrast, UI color, character names). The game lacks danger/safety signaling systems, faction symbols, or environmental storytelling beyond basic sports team identity. Data is sparse due to source material focusing on regional differences rather than gameplay visual systems.
